protein_name: May function as a specific light chain of unconventional myosin-10 (MYO10), also enhances MYO10 translation, possibly by acting as a chaperone for the emerging MYO10 heavy chain protein. May compete with calmodulin by binding, with different affinities, to cellular substrates. Belongs to the calmodulin family. Expressed in normal mammary, prostate, cervical, and epidermal tissues. It is greatly reduced or undetectable in transformed cells. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.

raw_fields.Molecular Function: calcium ion binding; metal ion binding; protein binding
raw_fields.Biological Process: calcineurin-mediated signaling; detection of calcium ion; regulation of release of sequestered calcium ion into cytosol by sarcoplasmic reticulum
raw_fields.Alt. Names/Synonyms: CALL3; CALML3; calmodulin like 3; calmodulin-like 3; Calmodulin-like protein 3; Calmodulin-related protein NB-1; CaM-like protein; CLP
raw_fields.Basal Isoelectric point: 4.3 Predict pI for various phosphorylation states
